programmes.show_description: The Bearded Lady: The very first episode of the mystery drama with Patricia Routledge. On her 60th birthday, Hetty Wainthropp decides to change her life and becomes a detective.
Random Acts: A woman hires Pearl to look into a large sum of cash left in her mailbox, the restauranteur-turned-detective finds that Whitstable may have a mysterious benefactor.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Hungry for Love: Comedy from Essex, Chigwell to be precise, as Sharon keeps a close eye on Tracey when she goes on a diet after a hurtful remark from husband Darryl.
Crime and Punishment: Hollis stumbles across some grave goings-on at a crematorium, while Brownlow confronts a ghost from his past.
New. The One: A teenage girl, inspired by her parents' perfect marriage, is convinced she's found 'the one' - until she makes a visit to the surgery.
New. Expectations: Archie and Melody argue over JJ's drug use, and Michelle finds herself in the middle of a surrogacy arrangement between two sisters involved with the same man.
Smoke and Mirrors: Mickie reconsiders her future following criticism from Mubbs, Jess decides to put her own feelings before her father's wishes and Owen reassesses his priorities.
The Lion Roars: Kirsty prepares to leave Warren, and the staff are devastated they failed to notice the extent of Ruth's mental health problems.
Crime and Punishment: Hollis stumbles across some grave goings-on at a crematorium, while Brownlow confronts a ghost from his past.
New. The Masoods are the talk of the Square. To Whitney's horror, Billie is determined to attend his army interview. Meanwhile, Liz returns from Spain.
New. A furious Qadim returns to the Square with a score to settle. Pat and Peggy prepare for their hot dates - with the same man! Becca hatches a plan, but will Fat Boy play ball?
Blood and Water: Timeless fun with the Yorkshire vets. James is caught in the middle of a 50-year war of silence between rabbit breeder Oliver Strong and cabbage grower Roland Strong.
Jack, Bill and Leo face stormy waters as they set sail together, while Sir Edward returns to Tarrant with business on his mind.
Eye Witness: Patricia 'Hyacinth Bucket' Routledge stars in the peculiar mystery series. Wainthropp Detective Agency is up and running, and Hetty must find a missing bird-watcher.
Tinker, Tailor: Comedy from the famed Essex siblings. Sharon begins to wonder if her husband was right when he told her there was a 'grass' planted in his cell.
Isometrics and After: More fun with the three old men of Yorkshire. Compo and Clegg are not as fit as they should be, according to Foggy, so they embark on a fitness regime.
Ferret Come Home: Vintage sitcom with some lovely scenery and the three aging Yorkshire lads. Compo's passion for Nora Batty clashes with his concern for his missing ferret.
Getting on Sidney's Wire: The long-running show with the three old devils of Yorkshire. The trio's attempts to do some handy work around the cafe are doomed to failure.
The Face of the Enemy: The Roman Catholic priest spies trouble when Lady Felicia returns to Kembleford from Africa, having split from her lover Benedict Northam. Mark Williams stars.
Dem Bones: It's the Great Slaughter Giant Marrow Contest, but before the judging can commence, a gruesome discovery is made in Edie's allotment plot... Lots of recent human bones!
Loyalties and Royalties: Those amiable detectives brush the dust off another unsolved mystery. When a guitarist is killed, Gerry gets to meet his heroes, rock band Bad Faith.
Limbo: A tough mystery to crack for DI Thomas Lynley and DS Barbara Havers. Old wounds are reopened when Lynley investigates the disturbing death of his godson.
Eye Witness: Patricia 'Hyacinth Bucket' Routledge stars in the peculiar mystery series. Wainthropp Detective Agency is up and running, and Hetty must find a missing bird-watcher.
Civil War: When a retired policeman is murdered during a Civil War re-enactment, the complicated investigation into his death uncovers three mistresses and a 35-year-old feud.
Lights, Camera, Murder!: A TV crew arrives to use St Vincent's Convent as a location for a spy thriller; the filming becomes all too real when a live gun is fired instead of a replica.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Time and Tides: Comedy from the east - Essex that is. Dorien is in need of all the loving support she can get from Sharon and Tracey; which doesn't sound very likely!
Zero Tolerance: Inspector Monroe clamps down on anti-social behaviour among the youth of Sun Hill, but others suffer as a result of his single-mindedness.
New. A Step Too Far: Nick and Jimmi come into conflict over the treatment of an agoraphobic mother and Melody discovers JJ is taking illegal drugs.
New. Endless Love: An old school friend of Ruth's tries to track down her father when her mother dies, while Archie struggles with his guilty conscience after discussing JJ with Eva.
Thicker than Water: Ric celebrates his 50th birthday, but tensions are running high at the party, as Jess tries to make Zubin jealous and Connie plays mind games with Will.
Place of Safety: Ruth is sectioned and held in a specialist unit, where consultant psychiatrist Andrew Brookfield tries to pinpoint the cause of her distress.
Zero Tolerance: Inspector Monroe clamps down on anti-social behaviour among the youth of Sun Hill, but others suffer as a result of his single-mindedness.
New. Zainab's deceit is finally revealed to a devastated Masood. Pat and Peggy each show off their new gentleman friend to a bewildered Dot.
New. Zainab fights to save her marriage as Tamwar makes a confession. Billy resorts to desperate measures to make ends meet and the identity of the Blue Masquerader is revealed.
Where Sheep May Safely Graze: Yorkshire countryside and intriguing veterinary dilemmas are on the cards. Alfred, the beloved cat of local confectioner Geoff Hatfield, falls ill.
Avril confronts Ken and Lynne returns home. Meanwhile, Sir Edward reveals shocking news about his health.
Fingers: Patricia Routledge leaves Hyacinth Bucket behind in the compelling mystery series. Hetty takes on a new case while on holiday with Robert. But who is she working for?
Baby Come Back: More jokes about Essex girls in the popular sitcom. When Sharon meets Dave, the telephone engineer and old flame, her thoughts turn to marriage and motherhood.
Jubilee: Vintage laughs from the three old fellas in the show that seems to be immortal. Foggy wants to do something for the Royal Jubilee, but things, as ever, don't go to plan.
Flower Power Cut: The loveable elderly trio of Yorkshiremen get into more trouble as Clegg is caught behaving oddly in the cafe.
Who Made a Bit of a Splash in Wales, Then?: Much-cherished sitcom. A trip to the valleys for the youthful codgers, as Foggy pays a visit to his lady friend.
New. Sparring with Death: Final Chapter: A second death at the Wildemarsh Wellness Retreat leads Mattie to uncover a heart-wrenching tragedy, waiting to be avenged.
The Devil You Know: Mallory is forced to turn to the sleuthing priest for help when a senior member of the police force is murdered at a bowls match.
Couldn't Organise One: Dennis 'Minder' Waterman stars in the crime drama with a splash of comedy. The team probes a death at a brewery, which proves an intoxicating experience.
Know Thine Enemy: Mystery with the aristocratic detective. Lynley and Havers's friendship is pushed to its limits in a case probing the disappearance of two teen girls.
